threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You ask for a coffee 'to go' and get a blank stare; it’s always 'here' unless you're off to the park.
Heard a bloke on the 35 last night trying to order a kebab from the driver. Thought he was joking until he actually started bargaining for it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I heard three different languages blending in the park, while a group was setting up for an outdoor movie night. Felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find spontaneous dance parties in the U-Bahn at 2 AM. It’s a wild mix of locals and tourists, all sharing the moment.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone's a local when they confidently step over a sleeping dog on the tracks without even breaking stride.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Nothing like waiting for the 19 in the Tenderloin while a jazz band sets up right next to the street. Makes the delays feel a bit less painful.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think the fountains are for drinking. But locals know better—they're just for show. No one wants a mouthful of that water.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll catch them staring at the metro maps like it's a treasure map, or trying to pay for paella in euros after ordering in English.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's a 'quick walk' to the next bus stop instead of a half-hour trek. Those little detours add up, trust me.
Caught a night bus from Brixton to Peckham, and there was a guy serenading the whole route. His off-key cover of 'Wonderwall' made the ride feel like a late-night dive bar.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the same guy at the corner kebab stand every Friday night, arguing passionately with the vendor over the price. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find spontaneous karaoke sessions in the middle of Warschauer Strasse at night. It's like the city's hidden talent just bursts out when the clock strikes midnight.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they calmly navigate a BART detour while muttering about the 14 Mission bus being late for the third time this week.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the bus at Civic Center just as they announced a delay due to an actual dog on the tracks. Only in SF can that be a Tuesday.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but miss the real charm in the small plazas where locals gather for vermut. That’s where the heart is.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them easily; just look for the ones still trying to navigate the metro map without Google Maps in hand.
